title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Harrison, NJ | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Harrison, NJ. Safe, humane bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Dealing with bats in your Harrison, NJ home or business? Best Pest Control NYC provides safe, humane bat removal and exclusion services throughout Harrison and the surrounding areas. Our licensed specialists use proven techniques to remove bats and prevent their return, protecting your property and health.
whyUs: With over a decade of experience serving Harrison and the greater NYC metro area, Best Pest Control NYC combines local expertise with cutting-edge exclusion methods. Our technicians are fully licensed, insured, and trained in humane bat control practices that comply with all New Jersey wildlife regulations.
localContext: Harrison's mix of historic Victorian homes and modern developments near the Passaic River creates ideal entry points for bats seeking shelter. From properties along Frank E. Rodgers Boulevard to residential areas near Harrison Park, we've helped countless Harrison residents reclaim their attics and chimneys from bat colonies.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Harrison, NJ?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Harrison typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and extent of exclusion work needed. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before any work begins.

faqs.question: Is bat removal in New Jersey regulated?
faqs.answer: Yes, bat removal in New Jersey is regulated by state wildlife laws. Bats are protected species and can only be excluded during specific times of year (typically outside of maternity season from May to August). Our licensed professionals ensure all work complies with NJ Department of Environmental Protection regulations.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Harrison home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, brown staining around small openings, and seeing bats entering or exiting your home at sunset. If you notice any of these signs in your Harrison property, contact us for a professional inspection.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our bat exclusion process includes a thorough inspection to identify all entry points, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, sealing all potential entry points, attic cleanup and sanitization if needed, and a warranty to ensure bats don't return. The process typically takes 2-4 weeks to complete.
